rationale: Tier 2 terminal không thể render trong chat nên web vẫn là kênh terminal duy nhất; ngược lại notify/verb có cấu trúc là thế mạnh Telegram và airemote đã dogfood trọn stack (auth im lặng D46/D56, wizard /new, poll-offset resume). Bỏ được 2 phần đắt/rủi ro của web: PWA web-push (phụ thuộc verify events) và provision form + auth flow.
alternatives: A: Telegram chỉ làm notify; C: Telegram-first, lùi web Tier 2. User chọn B ngày 2026-07-17.

decision: Chốt 3 open question PRD: Q1 provision hỗ trợ cả tạo-mới lẫn clone-URL, repo private mặc định; Q2 web auth = token tĩnh + cookie phiên, trước mắt 1 operator; Q4 web expose chỉ trong tailnet Tailscale, không public Internet.
rationale: Operator xác nhận 2026-07-17. Tailscale (WireGuard) đã mã hoá transport nên TLS thêm là tùy chọn; auth gate vẫn fail-closed làm defense-in-depth vì gateway là security boundary duy nhất trước herdr socket không auth.

decision: Kiến trúc backend: clean nhất có thể, hexagonal (ports & adapters) CHỈ tại nút thắt đã biết chắc có >=2 implementation thật: HerdrPort (CLI subprocess hôm nay ↔ JSON socket khi herdr chuyển server-owned), EventSource (poll 500ms ↔ events.subscribe theo kết quả PBI-001), Notifier/Channel (Telegram hôm nay, kênh khác sau — PRD §5.5 core channel-agnostic), Store (persistence nhỏ: offset + delivered). Security validators là pure function không cần port; Tier 2 relay là pipe trong suốt web↔HerdrPort không qua core layer; main.rs là composition root duy nhất làm injection.
rationale: User yêu cầu hệ thống clean, hexagon tại nút thắt để inject chức năng. YAGNI: hexagon toàn cục là ceremony — port chỉ đặt nơi swap là nhu cầu thật đã ghi trong PRD/backlog, không đặt phòng hờ.

decision: Chốt async stack backend: tokio + axum. Telegram Bot API gọi thẳng qua reqwest, KHÔNG dùng teloxide — bot chỉ cần getUpdates/sendMessage/editMessage/answerCallbackQuery/deleteWebhook, và poll offset phải do mình own (act → persist → fetch, quyết định Telegram-B) nên bypass polling loop của thư viện đúng pattern airemote D53. Fallback Go trong PRD chỉ kích hoạt nếu tokio tỏ ra quá rườm ở phần relay khi làm thật.
rationale: Relay WS + bot poll + supervisor timer chạy đồng thời cần async runtime; axum là chuẩn de-facto trên tokio cho WS. Teloxide kéo dependency lớn và tự own update loop — ngược yêu cầu offset caller-owned.

decision: HerdrPort tách làm 2 trait: HerdrControl (snapshot, workspace.create, agent start, ping/version, verb Tier 1) và HerdrStream (observe/control — stream frame + input). Tier 2 relay trong module web chỉ phụ thuộc HerdrStream; core/supervisor/watcher dùng HerdrControl. Cả hai do cùng adapter CLI implement hôm nay, socket JSON sau.
rationale: Tier 2 relay là pipe trong suốt cố tình không qua core — cho nó thấy nguyên HerdrPort là mở cửa gọi nhầm verb có side-effect từ đường relay. Tách trait làm hẹp bề mặt từng consumer, đúng tinh thần hexagon-tại-nút-thắt.

decision: Spike PBI-002 (Tier 2 observe/control relay): kiến trúc relay WebSocket↔herdr observe/control KHẢ THI ở protocol layer — herdr cấp đủ (frame seq-ordered, full=true frame đầu + khi resize, --takeover evict writer cũ, backpressure coalesce không stall/không hở seq, protocol pin exact-match=16). §10 [CAO] #2 de-risk ở lớp protocol; chỉ còn số liệu latency/UX thực cần live test sau khi có relay (§11 bước 4). 4 điểm khoá thiết kế: (1) seq chỉ để ORDER — relay không cần gap-recovery; (2) protocol pin exact-match, pin theo từng phiên bản herdr vendored, handshake lúc startup, mismatch = lỗi có kiểu; (3) IPC EOF thô KHÔNG phát terminal.closed → relay coi EOF như close (end WS, reconnect+full); (4) reconnect = full frame mới, không replay output đã lỡ.
rationale: Verify trực tiếp source upstreams/herdr (PROTOCOL_VERSION=16), có file:line anchor: client/mod.rs:950-985 (schema), render_ansi.rs:79-86 (full/diff), render_stream.rs:105-114 (seq không hở), headless.rs:2507-2529 (takeover), 2825-2886 (resize), 906-932 wire.rs (exact-match). Bằng chứng: .bee/spikes/tier2-observe-control/findings.md + docs/DISCOVERY.md.

decision: MỞ (cần operator chốt): trên phone xoay portrait↔landscape, gateway resize theo chế độ OBSERVER hay CONTROLLER? Observer resize chỉ đổi viewport riêng của phone (không đụng PTY thật, desktop client không bị ảnh hưởng); Controller resize gọi runtime.resize → đổi PTY thật system-wide (mọi client + chương trình thấy size mới). Cả hai đều ép full=true frame kế tiếp.
rationale: Source headless.rs:2836-2859 (controller→runtime.resize) vs 2860-2875 (observer→chỉ viewport riêng). Là product trade-off, không quyết được từ source: mobile-first WYSIWYG nghiêng controller; không quấy desktop client nghiêng observer. Chốt trước khi build §11 bước 4 (Tier 2 landscape terminal).

decision: PBI-001 spike verdict: herdr 0.7.4 events.subscribe LÀ đáng tin — Event watcher CÓ THỂ nâng poll 500ms → subscribe. Chốt: subscribe ack subscription_started rồi stream; socket là one-request-per-connection (subscribe giữ 1 connection riêng, mọi call khác là short-lived); subscribe replay ring buffer event gần đây (đo 93 event, giống hệt 2 lần) nên KHÔNG mất event khi reconnect nhưng BẮT BUỘC de-dup + reconcile snapshot; per-pane event (output_matched/agent_status_changed/scroll_changed) cần pane_id; error response không có id. Notify vẫn poll cho M0 (Telegram-B 302c0544). Residual: đo agent_status_changed latency + 2x/1ms với agent thật.
rationale: Verify sống theo PRD §10 [CAO] #1, gỡ khoá kiến trúc watcher. Bằng chứng .bee/spikes/pbi-001-events-subscribe/ + docs/DISCOVERY.md.
alternatives: Giữ poll 500ms mãi (an toàn nhưng bỏ lỡ độ trễ thấp của subscribe); subscribe không reconcile (sai — replay backlog gây double-notify).

decision: Resize khi xoay phone: HYBRID theo loại connection — chế độ XEM (observe) dùng observer resize (chỉ đổi viewport phone, không đụng PTY thật); chế độ GÕ (control, đã --takeover writer) dùng controller resize (runtime.resize → PTY thật reflow đúng cỡ phone). Cả hai đều nhận full=true frame kế tiếp để reset xterm.js.
rationale: Operator chốt 2026-07-17. Khớp ngữ nghĩa Tier 2 sẵn có: control = 'như ngồi tại máy' (phone đang là chỗ ngồi hiện tại), observe = nhiều thiết bị xem không phá nhau. Single operator nên xung đột desktop-đang-mở là hiếm và chấp nhận được khi chính mình đang gõ từ phone.

decision: herdr integration is a SOCKET CLIENT of the herdr server (peer to the TUI), talking herdr.sock's 85-command JSON API (protocol 16), not a herdr-binary subprocess wrapper. M1's CLI adapter + wire types + Fake were built from PRD prose and use names (terminal.observe/control/frame) that do not exist; real API is session.snapshot / pane.read / pane.send_input / events.subscribe / ping. Snapshot is a flat agents[] list, status set idle|working|blocked|done|unknown. Live-render mechanism and any single-writer/takeover lock are UNVERIFIED (no streaming request command; no takeover in the API) — need a herdr-source spike. herdr adapter/wire/relay/Fake need reality-alignment; rest of M1 stands. Full finding in docs/DISCOVERY.md 2026-07-18.
rationale: Live-probed herdr 0.7.4 (api schema, api snapshot, pane read) while trying to run against real herdr. User correctly reframed: we are a client of the server. Supersedes M1 Fake-derived herdr design.

decision: Fork C chốt: quan sát herdr thật bằng POLL pane.read trước (socket client), không chờ live-stream. rationale: pane.read poll đã đọc được TUI thật, thấp rủi ro, ra kết quả ngay; socket-stream phụ thuộc cơ chế live-render chưa verify. User chốt.

decision: Mobile terminal scrollback is a herd limitation, not fixable in the gateway via the JSON API
rationale: herdr.sock JSON pane.read returns only the current screen (max_offset=0 on alt-screen TUI agents; no scrollback/stream method). Clean fix = herd feature request to expose per-pane scrollback on the JSON API. Full evidence: docs/history/learnings/20260718-terminal-scrollback-herdr-limit.md

decision: new-shell-new-agent D1: FAB bottom-right on the switcher is the single entry point for both new shell and new agent
rationale: Creating is the apps only write action so it earns the thumb zone; the header already holds logout which makes a plus icon there a mis-tap hazard; a per-workspace plus has a dead zone at zero workspaces; snapshot focused_workspace_id removes the only real advantage of a contextual button

decision: new-shell-new-agent D2: one bottom sheet with a destination selector on top and action rows below, Shell first then one row per agent preset, not a two-step choose-then-configure menu
rationale: new shell needs zero params while agent.start requires name plus argv plus cwd; a shared configure step would make the cheap action pay the expensive actions price. Both stay at two taps

decision: new-shell-new-agent D3: destination is a single choice combining workspace and its folder, not two independent pickers
rationale: herdr derives the workspace label from its live folder at workspace.rs 1043-1076, so workspace and project folder are the same thing to the user; asking twice would invent a distinction the product does not have

decision: new-shell-new-agent D4: agent presets as label plus argv live in gateway config.json and are editable through doctor
rationale: herdr has no startable-agent registry: server.agent_manifests carries only screen-detection rule metadata with no argv and no display name, and agent.start takes caller-supplied argv. The list must be ours; config keeps it changeable without a release

decision: new-shell-new-agent D5: pass cwd explicitly on both tab.create and agent.start, resolved as foreground_cwd or else cwd of the destination workspaces focused pane
rationale: agent.start does not use the follow-cwd policy at all: with cwd omitted it falls back to the herdr server process current dir then slash at app/agents.rs 118-122, which would start an agent in the wrong repo silently. herdr docs confirm explicit cwd takes precedence over the terminal new_cwd policy, and the sheet already promised a folder so predictability beats config fidelity

decision: new-shell-new-agent D6: new shell uses tab.create not pane.split, and both create calls pass focus false while the mobile app navigates itself into the new panes terminal detail
rationale: pane.split requires a direction whose right/down meaning is empty on a phone that has no layout view and renders every pane as a full-screen card; tab.create has no required params and does not cram the desktop layout. focus false keeps a phone action from stealing the desktop viewport, while the phone still shows what it just made

decision: new-shell-new-agent D7: agent name is auto-generated and retried on the agent_name_taken error, never typed by the user
rationale: agent.start requires a globally unique name and rejects duplicates with agent_name_taken at app/agents.rs 208-253; typing a unique name on a phone keyboard is a punishment, and the collision is machine-detectable so the machine should resolve it

decision: new-shell-new-agent D8: slice 1 can only create inside workspaces that already exist; no directory browser and no workspace.create. The escape hatch to another folder is the existing Type panel, cd into it, and the destination follows
rationale: In herdr a workspaces folder is emergent, not chosen: the desktop cannot pick a project either, it spawns from the active panes cwd and you cd from there. cwd is read live from /proc so the cd lands immediately. A directory picker would make mobile a different product from the desktop and would add a new directory-listing endpoint and security surface for a case the Type panel already covers

decision: new-shell-new-agent D10: the anchor pane of any workspace is found by joining workspace_id to WorkspaceInfo.active_tab_id, then to the layouts entry matching both workspace_id and tab_id, then its focused_pane_id, then that pane in panes. The snapshot top-level focused ids are not used for this
rationale: Fresh-eyes review caught that focused_workspace_id, focused_tab_id, focused_pane_id and PaneInfo.focused are all global and describe only the one active workspace, so they leave every non-focused destination with no anchor. Each Workspace keeps its own active_tab index and per-tab layout focus independent of global focus, and both are wire-visible; herdr emits a layouts entry for every tab of every workspace. This join reproduces focused_pane_cwd_in_workspace exactly, which is what tab.create follows server-side when cwd is omitted

decision: new-shell-new-agent D11: when the anchor pane folder disagrees with the folder implied by the workspace label, the path wins and is what goes on the wire; planning must not reconcile the two
rationale: herdr computes them from different panes on purpose: the label comes from the first tabs root pane via resolved_identity_cwd_from while the create-follow comes from the active tabs focused pane via focused_pane_cwd_in_workspace. Once a user cds in one pane they legitimately diverge and no client-side reconciliation can be correct. Showing both with the path governing is the only honest presentation

decision: new-shell-new-agent: snapshot absence policy — panes and layouts join agents as hard malformed errors when absent, while workspaces and tabs keep their best-effort empty fallback
rationale: Settled while implementing cell new-shell-new-agent-1. herdr's schema marks panes and layouts required on SessionSnapshot, so their absence means a broken or older server rather than a normal empty case, and the protocol pin already rejects a mismatched server at ping. Extending the existing silent .ok() swallow to them would reproduce the 2026-07-18 population-sites incident, where an empty array shipped against a live herdr and nothing failed loudly

decision: tier is judged on the residual risk at dispatch time, not on the plan's original risk label; cell new-shell-new-agent-2 ran at generation despite plan.md rating the D10 join HIGH
rationale: By the time cell 2 dispatched, its design risk had been retired three times: D10 locked the exact join order, a live spike proved the join resolves against real multi-workspace data, and the cell text pinned the signature, the join order, the precedence rule and both degrade paths, leaving no design freedom. What remained was transcription against a pre-computed oracle, which is generation work. The reasoning was reconstructable from the artifacts but recorded nowhere, so a later reader comparing the risk map against the tier assignment would see an unexplained mismatch. Record the why next to the tier choice, not only in the cell text that happens to constrain the worker

decision: the project's standing verify now runs clippy with --all-targets, matching every CI job
rationale: Found during compounding: config.json recorded cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings while all three CI jobs run --all-targets. Only --all-targets compiles cfg(test) code, which is where most of a test-first cell's new code lives, so 48 of 81 cells were gated by a strictly weaker check than the one that blocks the merge. Aligning local with CI is strictly stronger and removes a whole class of pass-locally-fail-in-CI

decision: macos-installer-runtime-smoke-p1-fix: matrix jobs with needs: <matrix-job> in GitHub Actions gate on the matrix job's AGGREGATE conclusion, which is failure if ANY leg fails regardless of fail-fast setting -- fail-fast:false only controls whether sibling legs get cancelled early, not the final reported conclusion needs: checks. Any future job depending on one specific leg of a multi-leg matrix job needs either a dedicated non-matrixed job for that leg, or an explicit if: ${{ !cancelled() }} plus its own downstream failure detection (never bare needs: <matrix-job> alone) if it must tolerate unrelated leg failures.
rationale: This exact class of bug was independently found by 2 reviewers in the same review session and confirmed by direct testing; worth keeping as a durable repo-level GitHub Actions gotcha for any future job added downstream of the existing build matrix (e.g. a future Linux install-smoke test, or Intel Mac support per PBI-016).

decision: home list can be made pane-centric at zero cost in herdr calls: session.snapshot already returns panes[] alongside agents[], the gateway already parses it, and herdr's own UI does not filter workspaces by agent presence
rationale: Researched against the vendored herdr source and the captured protocol-16 schema. pane.list exists but is unnecessary: SessionSnapshot carries agents[] and panes[] side by side using the identical PaneInfo schema pane.list returns, so switching the list surface costs no new RPC, only iterating snap.panes instead of snap.agents and widening wire::Pane to parse fields already in the payload. The reason shells vanish is herdr's agent.list filter Terminal::is_agent_terminal, which requires an agent name, a detected agent label, or launch argv -- a plain shell has none. herdr's own TUI renders every workspace unconditionally, so the gateway is currently narrower than the tool it fronts, which makes this a defect rather than a design choice. Switching loses only AgentInfo's name and screen_detection_skipped fields

decision: Home lists shell panes too, and a shell gets a visually distinct card that carries NO status badge -- reusing the agent status scale for a shell would assert something false (unknown means 'herdr sent a value we do not recognise', not 'this pane has no agent'). Chosen over a shell-badge-on-the-same-card-shape variant and over leaving Home agent-only: both listing directions require updating docs/specs/switcher.md anyway (it currently describes a list of agents), so the card redesign is unavoidable once shells are listed at all. Knock-on: workspace rollup badge needs a no-agent form, and duplicate workspace labels stop being hypothetical.
rationale: User decision 2026-07-21 while resuming new-shell-new-agent. Design brief for an external designer written to plans/reports/design-brief-260721-1153-shell-card-on-home-screen.md

decision: HerdrError grows typed variants for exactly the three herdr error codes a caller BRANCHES on -- agent_name_taken (auto-generated name must be retried per D7), workspace_not_found (slice 4 stale destination), invalid_agent_argv (slice 3 preset validation) -- plus a catch-all Remote{code,message} that preserves the code string for every other upstream code. Request(String) keeps its current meaning: WE failed to make the request (serialize/connect/write/read), as opposed to the server refusing. Rejected one-variant-per-code (enum grows with every upstream code, and the other six codes have exactly one caller behaviour: display the message) and rejected pure Request{code,message} (loses compiler enforcement on the three codes the product actually acts on). parse_response today discards error.code entirely (socket.rs:205-221); that is the single line of loss this slice repairs. A response with no code maps to Remote with an empty code, never to Malformed -- turning a real refusal into 'malformed response' would hide the server's message.
rationale: Answers the open question plan.md:88 left to slice 2; the frozen exit state at plan.md:58 already names exactly those three codes as needing to be distinguishable

decision: The agent-name collision retry lives INSIDE the herdr port's agent_start, not in its callers: generate -> call -> on AgentNameTaken regenerate -> retry, bounded at 5 attempts with a distinct terminal error. Slice 4's HTTP layer should not have to know that names are auto-generated. The bound exists because a sixth collision means the name generator is broken, and looping harder would hide that.
rationale: D7 (CONTEXT.md:37) makes the name caller-generated and retried; placing the loop at the port keeps every future caller correct by construction

decision: A test substitute that is KINDER than the real path makes the whole suite blind, and it happened twice in one slice: FakeHerdr filled in WorkspaceNotFound.workspace_id while SocketHerdr left it empty, and FakeHerdr invented a tab id where real herdr would refuse. Both were green. The rule that catches it: whenever a fake and a live client answer the same question, the reviewer's job is to diff their ANSWERS, not just read each one -- any field the fake populates, the live path must populate; any shape the fake can produce, the host must be able to produce too. A fake whose failure modes are gentler than production's is a fake that certifies nothing.
rationale: Both defects survived a green worker verify and were caught only by orchestrator review comparing the two implementations against each other

decision: Do not borrow a status word that means 'we did not understand' to express 'there is nothing here'. docs/specs/switcher.md defines unknown as 'herdr reported a value this app does not recognize'; a freshly started agent got AgentStatus::Unknown, and separately the product decision about listing plain shells hit the identical trap. Both are the absence of a thing, not an unrecognized report of it. A fresh agent is idle (no work in progress -- true); a shell needs its own visual category, not a status at all.
rationale: Same category error surfaced twice in one day from unrelated directions, which is the signal it is a pattern rather than a slip

decision: Shell card on Home, settled 2026-07-21: a rounded-border rectangle with NO elevated grey fill -- it sits on the page background while agent cards sit on an elevated surface, which is what separates the two species at a glance. A shell icon on the left. Line 1 is the pane's folder, abbreviated to ~/... when it is under the home folder and shown in full otherwise. Line 2 is 'shell . {tab label}', mirroring the agent card's '{kind} . {tab label}'. NO status badge and no status colour anywhere on the card.
rationale: User decision after reviewing the design brief; the no-fill treatment carries the distinction without borrowing any status colour

decision: The shell PROGRAM (zsh/bash/fish/PowerShell) is not knowable from herdr today, so the shell card must not claim it. Verified in four places: our wire Pane type has no such field; herdr's captured protocol schema gives PaneInfo 19 fields with no shell/program/argv/command among them; herdr resolves a shell at spawn time (config -> $SHELL -> platform default, upstreams/herdr/src/pane.rs:1206-1226) but never attaches it to any serialized pane; and a real captured plain-shell pane carries only terminal_title, a free-text prompt string like 'dev@host:~/projects/forgent'. Inferring the shell from that prompt was rejected: it would be right most of the time and silently wrong sometimes, which is the failure class this project spent slice 2 removing. Line 2 therefore reads 'shell . {tab label}'. Asking herdr upstream for a real field stays open as a later option.
rationale: Feasibility check before committing the design; an honest gap beats a plausible guess

decision: auto-approved Gate 1 (bypass): agent-presets-config context locked with P1-P6, the headline being that an empty or malformed agent argv is a CONFIG-LOAD FAILURE, not a preset that renders disabled. Chosen because the frozen plan's own exit criterion says so, because the config layer already fails closed everywhere else (unknown key, empty allowed_roots, token-in-config all refuse), and because a disabled row is a failure the operator only meets on a phone away from the machine. The counter-argument (a bad preset stops the gateway booting, and the gateway is how the operator reaches everything) was weighed and outweighed: doctor already backs up, diagnoses and repairs an invalid config field by field, so a config that cannot boot is loud and tooled for, while a preset that quietly does nothing is neither.
rationale: gate_bypass full; the question was explicitly deferred to slice 3's planning and is answered from repo precedent rather than preference

decision: auto-approved Gate 3 (bypass): agent-presets-config execution approved. Feasibility established by direct code inspection rather than a spike: allowed_roots already demonstrates a list-valued setting end to end across all seven touch sites, and write.rs:165-171 rebuilds the config document from CONFIG_FIELDS alone, which is the mechanism behind the known trap (a field absent from that list is silently dropped on every doctor-driven save). The one thing without precedent is a list of two-field objects rather than a list of strings, which changes only the add prompt and the removal selector.
rationale: gate_bypass full; baseline verify green this session (201 Rust tests, fmt, clippy -D warnings, rename contract, web bundle + 20 tests)

decision: Cell boundaries must follow COUPLING, not module boundaries. agent-presets-config was split as config-layer / doctor-editor, which looked clean and was wrong: the doctor menu is generated from CONFIG_FIELDS, so growing that list from 8 to 9 shifted the menu's stop index and broke seven doctor tests that hardcoded it. The config cell could not satisfy 'register the field', 'keep all tests green' and 'do not touch doctor' at once. The tell to look for at planning time: does anything DERIVE from the thing I am changing -- a length, an index, a generated menu, a count in a doc or a test name? If yes, that derived site belongs in the same cell no matter which module it lives in.
rationale: Worker correctly reported BLOCKED rather than working around it; the fix was to widen scope and to derive the index from CONFIG_FIELDS.len() so the fragility is removed rather than moved

decision: web-create-endpoints (slice 4) context locked with P1-P9; Gate 1 auto-approved under gate_bypass=full. The destination list is assembled in the backend; a destination is {workspace_id, label, path, path_is_live}; a stale destination is 409; the phone names an agent preset by label and never sends argv.
rationale: The Windows destination question was reframed from a platform branch into data provenance. 'foreground_cwd is unix-only' invited a cfg(windows) fork in a layer that has none; the real predicate is which field the path came from, and unix hits the same fallback whenever foreground_cwd is absent. Blocking on Windows was rejected (unusable for a path that is usually right); label-only was rejected (discards a usually-right path); showing the fallback as certain is exactly what parent D5 forbids. 409-not-404 because 404 is load-bearing as the opaque unauthenticated answer, and the sheet must distinguish 'that workspace closed' from 'log in again'. Backend assembly because herdr's agents[] structurally omits shell-only workspaces and the anchor join already exists in Rust (wire.rs:242).

decision: auto-approved Gate 2 (bypass): web-create-endpoints (slice 4) shaped as 4 sequential cells across 8 product files, deviating from the frozen parent plan's 3-file scope by adding src/herdr/wire.rs and the port's three population sites.
rationale: Two facts only visible with slices 1-2 built. (a) anchor_cwd_for_workspace returns a bare Option<String> and discards which field the path came from, which is exactly what P2's path_is_live needs; recomputing it in the web layer would re-walk the same four-hop join in a second place with a second chance to disagree. (b) herdr's tab.create takes cwd as OPTIONAL and resolves the workspace's own anchor when it is absent (upstreams/herdr/src/app/api/tabs.rs:65-67), while our port declares cwd: &str and cannot express absent -- so a workspace whose anchor join misses would be either uncreatable or created at the empty path. Widening to Option<&str> makes the miss degrade into what the herdr desktop already does. The parent plan's exit state is unchanged and remains what binds. Cell boundaries follow coupling, not modules: the AppState::new signature change carries its derived sites (main.rs, test_state) inside the same cell.

decision: P10 (found by validation, after Gate 1): omitting cwd is safe on tab.create and unsafe on agent.start, so the shell route omits it when the destination path does not resolve and the agent route refuses with 409 instead. The plan's claim that herdr 'seeds the folder the same way the desktop would' for both verbs is false and is corrected in the cells; plan.md itself stays frozen.
rationale: tab.create with no cwd resolves the workspace's own anchor (upstreams/herdr/src/app/api/tabs.rs:65-67 -> resolve_new_terminal_cwd), which is precisely what the herdr desktop does. agent.start with no cwd falls back to std::env::current_dir of the HERDR SERVER PROCESS (upstreams/herdr/src/app/agents.rs:118-122) -- an arbitrary folder unrelated to the workspace. Symmetry was assumed from the tab.create reading and was wrong. Starting an agent in an arbitrary folder is exactly the silent wrong-repo start parent D5 forbids, and it would have been invisible in tests because FakeHerdr never modelled the fallback at all.

decision: Validation moved the fake-fidelity work into cell 2 (which owns src/herdr/fake.rs) and made cell 3 depend on it; AppState grows a field rather than an AppState::new parameter.
rationale: Four unprovable must-haves, all from the fake being kinder and narrower than the live client: every seeded pane sets foreground_cwd == cwd so path_is_live:false was unreachable though the live capture proves cwd-only panes are real (socket.rs:552-566); every seeded workspace has agents so the shell-only workspace this feature exists for could not be exercised; the fake returned WorkspaceNotFound where live returns agent_placement_not_found; and FakeHerdr exposes no seed mutator, so a web test cannot shape a snapshot at all. Separately, AppState::new has a THIRD caller in tests/observe_reply_e2e.rs:13, outside the cell's file scope -- a signature change would have reddened the cell's own verify on a file it is forbidden to touch, so the preset list arrives as a field instead.

decision: auto-approved Gate 3 (bypass): web-create-endpoints slice 4 execution approved, 4 sequential cells.
rationale: Feasibility established by an independent read-only validation pass, not a spike, and the pass earned its keep: it found a correctness defect (agent.start's omit-cwd fallback is the herdr process directory, not the workspace anchor -> P10 splits the two routes' unresolved-path branch), four fake-fidelity gaps that would have left the slice's headline must-haves unprovable while the suite stayed green, and a third AppState::new call site outside the cells' file scope. All seven folded into the cells. All four verify commands executed against the current tree and confirmed red, so none of them can pass on an untouched worktree. Baseline verify green before the work. No advisor is configured (AO2(b)); the fact is recorded in docs/history/web-create-endpoints/reports/validation-slice-4.md.

decision: Never infer one herdr API verb's fallback/error behavior from a sibling verb's without reading both implementations independently (generalized from P10).
rationale: web-create-endpoints validation proved tab.create and agent.start diverge on omitted cwd, contradicting the parent plan's assumed symmetry; a fake that models only one of a pair is itself a signal the pair was never compared. Promoted to critical-patterns.md.

decision: Before scoping a cell's file-touch boundary around a shared constructor (e.g. AppState::new), enumerate every caller first, including tests/*.rs integration files, not just src/.
rationale: web-create-endpoints cell 3 validation found a third AppState::new caller (tests/observe_reply_e2e.rs) outside the cell's declared scope; a naive signature change would have reddened the cell's own verify on a file it was forbidden to touch. Fixed via a builder method (with_agent_presets) instead of a constructor change.

decision: web-create-sheet S1: destination rows get a short disambiguating suffix (last chars of workspace_id) only when their label+path collides with another currently-listed destination; unique rows are unchanged.
rationale: Two workspaces sharing label+path is real and observed (herdr-port.md Open Gaps). workspace_id is already in the /api/create-options payload, so this is pure display-layer work, no backend change. User chose this over deferring or blocking on backend work.

decision: web-create-sheet S2 (auto-locked, gate_bypass=full): a destination row whose path is null or path_is_live is false gets an inline visual caveat on the row itself; no blocking confirmation dialog before creating.
rationale: P2/P8 already made path uncertainty travel as data, not a platform branch or a gate; D2 rejects multi-step flows. The actual safety guardrail for the dangerous case (agent.start into an unresolved folder) is already enforced server-side as a 409 refusal (P10), so a client-side confirmation would be redundant ceremony in a single-operator trusted app.

decision: web-create-sheet S3 (auto-locked, gate_bypass=full): a create-call error (409/400/502) is shown inline inside the still-open sheet, never as a toast that dismisses the sheet.
rationale: D2's two-tap goal is defeated if an error forces the user to reopen the sheet and re-pick a destination/preset. Inline keeps selection state and lets the user retry or pick another destination immediately.

decision: web-create-sheet S4 (auto-locked, gate_bypass=full): the FAB is disabled (not hidden) whenever the switcher's existing health indicator shows herdr unreachable.
rationale: Reuses the health-dot signal the switcher already computes every load; creating anything is pointless when herdr cannot be reached, and disabling (vs hiding) keeps the layout stable.

rationale: Fresh-eyes review found a plain shell can never produce an AgentRow (P1 of web-create-endpoints), so parent D6's navigate-after-create requirement was unimplementable against the original CONTEXT.md's Integration Points. herdr-port.md R14 guarantees the pane is immediately readable, so no poll-and-wait is needed; exact type shape stays deferred to planning.

decision: herdr-go service status (Linux) uses systemctl --user show --property=... not is-active
rationale: is-active encodes active/inactive in its own process exit code, which would make a merely-stopped-but-registered unit look like the *command* failed. show always exits 0 once the systemd user bus answers and prints state as text, keeping status's exit code consistently mean 'did the query succeed' -- symmetric with start/stop/restart reporting 'did the action succeed'. Any future consumer of this exit code (scripting, or a later web/API exposure per PBI-036) must preserve this: non-zero means the query failed, never 'service happens to be stopped'.
alternatives: systemctl --user is-active herdr-go.service (rejected -- exit code conflates query success with active state)

decision: health-fingerprint: build.rs no longer emits any cargo:rerun-if-changed directive (removed the static-only scoping) so it always reruns -- future build.rs edits must not reintroduce a scoped rerun-if-changed without re-solving the fingerprint staleness problem it would reopen.
